template.name: LeaderRobot
FollowerRobot
Environment

query.comment:
Property 1 (CRITICAL): Deadlock Freedom The entire system must never reach a state where no transitions are possible (even after time elapse). This is the most important property for any concurrent system.
Expected result: SATISFIED If violated: there exists a reachable state where both robots are waiting for the other to act first (mutual deadlock).

Property 2: Reachability of Normal Operation There exists at least one execution where both robots simultaneously reach their "normal" cooperative operation states.
Expected result: SATISFIED If violated: the normal operating mode is unreachable (system design error).

Property 3: Follower Eventually Follows (Liveness) Whenever the follower is in APPROACHING state, it will eventually reach FOLLOWING state. This ensures the approaching phase terminates.
Expected result: SATISFIED If violated: follower can approach forever without entering following mode.

Property 4: Leader Recovers from Dead End (Liveness) Whenever the leader detects a dead end, it will eventually return to EXPLORING state. This ensures dead ends don't permanently block.
Expected result: SATISFIED If violated: the leader can get permanently stuck in DEAD_END or REVERSING.

Property 5: Follower Finds Leader (Liveness) Whenever the follower is searching for the leader, it will eventually transition to APPROACHING state (meaning leader was found).
Expected result: SATISFIED (bounded by t <= 90 invariant + environment) If violated: follower can search forever without finding leader.

Property 6: Dead End State is Reachable Confirms that the dead end scenario (set up by the Environment template) is actually exercised. If this fails, the model is not testing what we think.
Expected result: SATISFIED

Property 7: Temporary Leader Role is Reachable The follower can reach the LEADING_TEMPORARILY state. This verifies the dead-end recovery protocol is fully reachable in the model.
Expected result: SATISFIED

query.formula: A[] not (Leader.WAITING_FOR_LOST_FOLLOWER and Follower.SEARCHING_FOR_LEADER and Leader.t >= 120)
query.comment:
Property 8: No Mutual Timeout (Livelock Prevention) The system must not remain in a state where both robots are simultaneously waiting/searching beyond the maximum timeout. The clock invariants (leader ≤ 120, follower ≤ 90) bound this.
Expected result: SATISFIED If violated: the timeout mechanism is broken, allowing infinite mutual wait.

Property 9: Waiting for Follower is Time-Bounded The leader must not wait indefinitely for the follower to approach. The clock invariant enforces this, but this query double-checks it.
Expected result: SATISFIED

Property 10: Search Phase is Time-Bounded The follower must not search indefinitely. After 90 seconds it transitions to ERROR, allowing operator intervention.
Expected result: SATISFIED

query.formula: Leader.EXPLORING --> (Leader.EXPLORING or Leader.DEAD_END or Leader.WAITING_FOR_FOLLOWER or Leader.WAITING_FOR_LOST_FOLLOWER)
query.comment:
Property 11: Progress from EXPLORING (Safety) Once the leader is exploring, it either continues exploring or transitions to a well-defined state. It cannot silently disappear.
Expected result: SATISFIED

Property 12 (ADVANCED - may NOT be satisfied): Universal Liveness ALL executions eventually reach the cooperative state. This is a stronger property than E<>. May fail due to ERROR states. Useful for students to analyze WHY it fails and what assumptions would make it hold.
Expected result: May fail (ERROR states are deadlocked) Exercise: Add assumptions to make this hold (e.g., no ERROR state).
